flows.name: MTA-test2
flows.description: Triage workflow for UW CoMotion Material Transfer Agreements (MTAs), guiding staff through agreement type identification and routing.

content.description: This flow triages incoming Material Transfer Agreement (MTA) requests at UW CoMotion, determining whether materials are incoming or outgoing, identifying the agreement type, and routing to the appropriate action.

content.question: Direction of Material Transfer

answers.text: Incoming: UW is receiving materials from an external party
Outgoing: UW is providing materials to an external party

content.question: Incoming MTA Agreement Type

answers.text: Provider uses UWMTA or NIH Simple Letter Agreement
Provider requires their own custom agreement

content.question: Outgoing MTA Agreement Type

answers.text: For profit: Recipient accepts UBMTA or UW standard terms
Non-profit: Recipient requests custom or negotiated terms

nodes.label: Execute Incoming MTA Using Standard Template

content.title: Execute Incoming MTA Using Standard Template
content.description: Use the UBMTA Letter of Implementation or NIH Simple Letter Agreement. Confirm PI details, material description, and permitted use. Route for signature to authorized UW signatory.

nodes.label: Review and Negotiate Provider Custom Incoming MTA

content.title: Review and Negotiate Provider Custom Incoming MTA
content.description: Review provider-supplied agreement for IP ownership, publication rights, and permitted use clauses. Negotiate terms with provider and route for legal review if needed before UW authorized signature.

nodes.label: Execute Outgoing MTA Using UW Standard or UBMTA Terms

content.title: Execute Outgoing MTA Using UW Standard or UBMTA Terms
content.description: Prepare the outgoing MTA using UW standard outgoing template or UBMTA Letter of Implementation. Confirm material description, recipient PI, and permitted use. Obtain PI approval and UW authorized signature.

nodes.label: Review and Negotiate Recipient Custom Outgoing MTA Terms

content.title: Review and Negotiate Recipient Custom Outgoing MTA Terms
content.description: Evaluate recipient-proposed custom terms for IP, publication, and sublicensing provisions. Negotiate to protect UW interests, involve CoMotion legal counsel as needed, and route for authorized UW signature.

flows.name: UW_CoMotion_MTA_Triage_Rationale
flows.description: Triage flow for UW CoMotion Material Transfer Agreement (MTA) requests, guiding staff through categorization and assignment of incoming MTA inquiries.

content.description: This flow triages incoming Material Transfer Agreement (MTA) requests at UW CoMotion, determining whether the transfer is incoming or outgoing, identifying the appropriate agreement type, and routing the request to the correct team for execution.
resources: UW Administrative Policy Statements on Research Agreements

content.question: Is the material being transferred into UW or out of UW?

answers.text: Incoming (external party sending material to UW)
Outgoing (UW sending material to external party)

content.question: What type of incoming MTA is applicable?

answers.text: Standard UBMTA or Simple Letter Agreement

content.question: Is the recipient a commercial entity or an academic/non-profit institution?

content.title: Execute Standard Incoming MTA (UBMTA or SLA)
content.description: Complete and execute the Uniform Biological Material Transfer Agreement (UBMTA) or Simple Letter Agreement for the incoming material. Confirm PI approval, collect required signatures, and file the executed agreement in CoMotion records.

content.title: Review and Negotiate Non-Standard Incoming MTA
content.description: Assign to a CoMotion licensing associate to review the provider-drafted agreement, negotiate terms protecting UW IP and publication rights, obtain legal review as needed, and execute upon approval.

content.title: Execute Outgoing MTA to Academic or Non-Profit Recipient
content.description: Prepare outgoing UBMTA or Simple Letter Agreement for transfer of UW material to an academic or non-profit institution. Obtain PI and CoMotion signatory approval and file the executed agreement.

content.title: Execute Outgoing MTA to Commercial Entity
content.description: Assign to a CoMotion licensing associate to draft or review a commercial outgoing MTA. Ensure appropriate IP protections, commercialization rights, and fee structures are addressed. Route for legal and leadership approval before execution.

materials.label: UW IP and Commercialization Rights Summary

flows.name: UW_CoMotion_MTA_Triage_Rationale
flows.description: Triage workflow for Material Transfer Agreements at UW CoMotion to determine appropriate handling and routing based on agreement characteristics

content.description: This workflow triages incoming Material Transfer Agreements to determine the appropriate review path and handling requirements based on material type, transfer direction, and institutional risk factors

nodes.label: Is this an incoming or outgoing MTA?

content.question: Determine the direction of material transfer

nodes.label: Does the incoming material involve biological hazards or controlled substances?

content.question: Assess material risk level and regulatory requirements

nodes.label: Does the outgoing material contain UW intellectual property?

content.question: Determine if IP protection terms are needed

nodes.label: Route to EH&S and Compliance Review

content.title: High-Risk Incoming MTA Review
content.description: Route agreement to Environmental Health & Safety for biosafety review and compliance verification before final approval

content.title: Standard Incoming MTA Approval
content.description: Review and approve standard incoming MTA using expedited process with standard terms and conditions

content.title: Outgoing MTA with IP Protection
content.description: Route to CoMotion IP team for review and inclusion of appropriate intellectual property protection clauses and publication restrictions

content.title: Standard Outgoing MTA Approval
content.description: Process outgoing MTA using standard template with basic research use restrictions and no special IP terms

flows.name: UW_CoMotion_MTA_Triage_Rationale
flows.description: Triage workflow for UW CoMotion Material Transfer Agreements (MTAs), guiding staff through the classification and routing of incoming and outgoing MTA requests.

content.description: This workflow supports the triage of Material Transfer Agreement (MTA) requests at the University of Washington through CoMotion. It helps determine the type of MTA, the direction of transfer, applicable templates, and the appropriate office or team to handle the agreement.
resources: UW Administrative Policy Statements on Research Agreements

nodes.label: Is the UW sending materials to an external party (Outgoing) or receiving materials from an external party (Incoming)?

content.question: Is the UW sending materials to an external party (Outgoing) or receiving materials from an external party (Incoming)?

answers.text: Outgoing – UW is providing materials to an external party
Incoming – UW is receiving materials from an external party

nodes.label: Is the external party receiving UW materials a non-profit research institution or academic organization?

content.question: Is the external party receiving UW materials a non-profit research institution or academic organization?

nodes.label: Is the biological material eligible for transfer under the Uniform Biological Material Transfer Agreement (UBMTA), and is the receiving institution a UBMTA signatory?

content.question: Is the biological material eligible for transfer under the Uniform Biological Material Transfer Agreement (UBMTA), and is the receiving institution a UBMTA signatory?

answers.text: Yes – Material and institution are UBMTA-eligible

nodes.label: Is the external provider requiring UW to sign their own MTA template, or will UW use its standard incoming MTA?

content.question: Is the external provider requiring UW to sign their own MTA template, or will UW use its standard incoming MTA?

answers.text: Yes – Provider requires use of their own MTA template
No – UW standard incoming MTA template will be used

nodes.label: Does the provider's MTA include terms that deviate from UW standard positions (e.g., IP assignment, publication restrictions, reach-through rights)?

content.question: Does the provider's MTA include terms that deviate from UW standard positions (e.g., IP assignment, publication restrictions, reach-through rights)?

answers.text: Yes – Non-standard or problematic terms identified
No – Terms are acceptable as presented

nodes.label: Execute UBMTA Implementing Letter for Outgoing Transfer

content.title: Execute UBMTA Implementing Letter for Outgoing Transfer
content.description: The material qualifies for transfer under the UW MTA. Prepare and execute a UBMTA Implementing Letter. Confirm the receiving institution is a current UBMTA signatory. Route for PI and authorized institutional signature. File executed letter in CoMotion records system.

nodes.label: Prepare Custom Outgoing MTA for Non-Profit/Academic Recipient

content.title: Prepare Custom Outgoing MTA for Non-Profit/Academic Recipient
content.description: The material is not eligible for UBMTA or the institution is not a signatory. Draft a custom outgoing MTA using the UW standard outgoing template. Negotiate terms as needed with the receiving institution. Obtain PI approval and route for authorized institutional signature through CoMotion.

nodes.label: Negotiate and Execute Outgoing MTA with Commercial/For-Profit Entity

content.title: Negotiate and Execute Outgoing MTA with Commercial/For-Profit Entity
content.description: The receiving party is a commercial or for-profit entity. This requires additional review of IP, licensing, and commercialization terms. CoMotion licensing staff will lead negotiation. Engage UW General Counsel if needed. Ensure terms protect UW IP rights and comply with any sponsor restrictions on the material.

nodes.label: Review and Negotiate Non-Standard Incoming MTA Terms

content.title: Review and Negotiate Non-Standard Incoming MTA Terms
content.description: The provider's MTA contains non-standard terms that require negotiation. CoMotion staff will work with the provider to revise problematic clauses (e.g., IP assignment, publication restrictions, reach-through rights). Engage UW General Counsel or OSP as appropriate. Document all redlines and obtain PI acknowledgment before execution.

nodes.label: Execute Incoming MTA Using Provider Template (Acceptable Terms)

content.title: Execute Incoming MTA Using Provider Template (Acceptable Terms)
content.description: The provider's MTA terms are acceptable under UW standard positions. Obtain PI signature confirmation, route for authorized institutional signature, and execute the agreement. File the executed MTA in CoMotion records and notify the PI and provider of completion.

nodes.label: Execute Incoming MTA Using UW Standard Incoming Template

content.title: Execute Incoming MTA Using UW Standard Incoming Template
content.description: The provider has agreed to use the UW standard incoming MTA template. Complete the template with material description, PI information, and permitted use details. Route for PI review and authorized institutional signature. Provide executed copy to the provider and file in CoMotion records.

content.question: What kind of agreement is this?
Does it involve human subjects?

content.title: Route to IRB review
content.description: Send the agreement package to the IRB coordinator before legal routing.

content.title: Route to CoMotion legal
content.description: Prepare the standard agreement packet for CoMotion legal review.

materials.label: Statement of work or project summary

content.title: Manual triage
content.description: Ask an agreement specialist to classify the document and confirm next steps.

flows.name: Smoke Test MTA Flow
flows.description: Material Transfer Agreement. If human subjects are involved, route to IRB. Otherwise route to CoMotion legal. If unknown, manual triage.

content.description: Material Transfer Agreement. If human subjects are involved, route to IRB. Otherwise route to CoMotion legal. If unknown, manual triage.
